client.name: VetsBoats Foundation
client.mission: Adaptive sailing for veterans in the SF Bay Area using restored wooden boats

foundations.name: St. Francis Sailing Foundation
foundations.dossier_notes: Mission includes 'disabled access to the sport of sailing.' Recent grants: $75K to TISC, $65K to StFYC Junior Sailing. Quarterly review cycle (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct). Bay Area based, same waters as VetsBoats. SFSF board overlaps heavily with TISC and StFYC leadership. Warm intro through sailing network recommended before applying.
foundations.fit_evidence: Funds disabled sailors in Bay Area. $75K to Treasure Island Sailing Center (2023). Mission explicitly includes disabled access to sailing. 3 grants totaling $151K, all in CA.
foundations.positioning_notes: Lead with adaptive sailing for veterans with disabilities. Reference TISC as comparable Bay Area sailing program they already fund. Emphasize waterfront location, hands-on programming, and wooden boat restoration as unique differentiator. Seek warm intro to Rolf Kaiser (President) or Maureen Roddy (Grants Chair) before submitting.

foundations.name: Kim & Harold Louie Family Foundation
foundations.dossier_notes: Gives 'special consideration to all military members and their families' — founder's father was a WWII veteran. 27 veteran-focused grants totaling $212,500 to orgs like Operation First Response, Semper Fi Fund, Fisher House. No restriction on private foundation applicants. Located in Burlingame, CA — 15 miles from VetsBoats. Typical grants $5K-$15K.
foundations.fit_evidence: 27 veteran grants totaling $212K. Military preference in mission. No PF exclusion. 15 miles from VetsBoats.
foundations.positioning_notes: Lead with veteran mission and local Bay Area presence. Reference military connection (founder's father WWII vet). Keep LOI concise — family foundation, personal touch matters.

foundations.name: Charles H. Stout Foundation
foundations.dossier_notes: Funds adaptive recreation programs for disabled veterans. Open to 501(c)(3) organizations without public charity restriction. Confirmed open application process with firm June 15 deadline. National scope.
foundations.fit_evidence: Adaptive recreation for disabled veterans is core mission. No PF exclusion. Open application confirmed.
foundations.positioning_notes: Frame VetsBoats as adaptive recreation (sailing) serving disabled veterans. Keep letter concise and mission-focused. Emphasize therapeutic and rehabilitation benefits of sailing.

foundations.name: Kovler Family Foundation
foundations.dossier_notes: 'No Restrictions' in application requirements — strongest signal that PFs are welcome. Funds Judd Goldman Adaptive Sailing Foundation ($5K/yr for multiple years), a Chicago adaptive sailing program very similar to VetsBoats. Also has 3 confirmed grants to other private foundations.
foundations.fit_evidence: Funds Judd Goldman Adaptive Sailing ($5K/yr). 'No Restrictions' on applicants. 3 confirmed PF grantees.
foundations.positioning_notes: Reference Judd Goldman Adaptive Sailing — VetsBoats is doing similar work on the West Coast. Call first (312-664-5050) to introduce before submitting proposal.

foundations.name: Howard Family Foundation
foundations.application_method: Contact Jason Danz at CIBC (bank trustee)
foundations.dossier_notes: Only foundation found that simultaneously funds adaptive sailing (Judd Goldman, 5+ consecutive yrs), mainstream sailing (Columbia Sailing School $80K), AND a veteran organization (Wounded Warrior Project, 3+ yrs). Bank-managed trust through CIBC. 12% of grantees are new each year.
foundations.fit_evidence: Funds Judd Goldman Adaptive Sailing (5+ yrs), Columbia Sailing School ($80K), AND Wounded Warrior Project (3+ yrs). Unique sailing + veterans combination.
foundations.positioning_notes: Pitch as 'Judd Goldman meets Wounded Warrior' — adaptive sailing specifically for veterans. Bank-managed foundations move slowly — 3-6 month cultivation.
